rulings.Case Title: WALTER CONNOLLY VS. 888 SEVENTH STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 31, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER AND ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Late-filed opposition is stricken. The request for temporary restraining order is denied. Moving party failed to show the requisite emergency. Request to issue an Order to Show Cause Why Preliminary Injunction should not be issued is granted. Moving papers for the preliminary injunction shall be filed and served no later than September 5, 2023. Hearing shall be set for October 3, 2023. Opposition shall be filed and served no later than September 19, 2023. Reply shall be filed and served no later than September 26, 2023. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TONY H.L. CHIANG ET AL VS. ROBERT C.M. CHIANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 31, 2023 line 3. HEARING ON ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION Hearing Required. No later than 9:00 a.m. on August 31, 2023 each party to submit a declaration stating: (1) whether Plaintiffs, Defendants, or a third party currently manages the property - collects rent, addresses tenant's issues/requests, etc.; (2) whether Plaintiffs, Defendants or a third party is currently responsible for remitting payments for mortgage, taxes, insurance, other recurring property expenses; (3) the amount and the due date of all property expenses, e.g. mortgage (the first of each month); taxes (annually on the first of February), insurance (every 3 month), e, etc.; (4) a copy of the most current bills for recurring property expenses referenced above, including any utilities not paid for by the tenants; (5) a copy of the most recent payments for mortgage, taxes, insurance, utilities, etc; and (6) a copy of a management agreement, if any; or if a management agreement is oral, the terms of such agreement. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
